title: T-003 — GS1-128 parser utility lib/utils/gs1-parser.ts (06-a)
prompt:
# T-003 — GS1-128 parser utility

PRD: 06-SCANNER-P1-PRD.md §8.1 FR-SC-BE-010, §10.3, §15.2 V-SCAN-INPUT-001..003
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Ship a deterministic GS1-128 parser used by every scanner workflow: GTIN-14 mod-10 check, AI extraction (01/10/17/21/310x/3103/3922), variable-length AI handling via Group Separator (ASCII 29), Y2K date handling per GS1 spec, and graceful fallback for unknown AIs.

## Implementation contract
1. New module `packages/scanner-utils/src/gs1-parser.ts` exporting:
   - `parseGS1(input: string): ParsedGS1` (per §10.3 type)
   - `validateGTIN14(gtin: string): boolean` (mod 10)
   - `detectBarcodeType(input: string): 'gs1-128' | 'code-128' | 'manual' | 'unknown'`
2. Y2K rule: YYMMDD → ISO 8601, YY<50 → 20YY else 19YY.
3. Variable-length AIs terminated by GS (\x1d) or end-of-string.
4. Unknown AI captured into `unknownAIs[]` with raw payload, never throws.
5. Vitest fixtures: ≥20 cases per AI 01/10/17/21/310x/3103/3922 covering: missing GS, invalid checksum, leading-zero weight, mixed case, UTF-8 batch.

## Files
- packages/scanner-utils/src/gs1-parser.ts [create]
- packages/scanner-utils/src/index.ts [modify]
- packages/scanner-utils/__tests__/gs1-parser.test.ts [create]
- packages/scanner-utils/__tests__/fixtures/gs1-samples.json [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given an input '0110012345678902' '10B20260410' GS '17260501' '3103000184', when parseGS1 runs, then it returns gtin='10012345678902', batch='B20260410', expiry=Date('2026-05-01T00:00:00Z'), netWeightKg=18.4 and valid=true
2. Given a GTIN with bad mod-10 check digit, when validateGTIN14 runs, then it returns false and parseGS1 sets valid=false with errors[] containing 'invalid_gtin_checksum'
3. Given an unknown AI like '99XYZ', when parseGS1 runs, then valid=true and unknownAIs contains '99' with raw payload preserved

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/scanner-utils/__tests__/gs1-parser.test.ts with ≥20 fixtures per primary AI before implementation exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter scanner-utils test gs1-parser'

## Risk red lines
- Do not silently mutate the original input (return immutable ParsedGS1)
- Do not throw on unknown AI (graceful degradation per §10.3)
- Do not introduce dependency on the @zxing camera library here

pipeline_inputs.description: Shared GS1-128 parser + GTIN-14 mod-10 validator + barcode-type detector with vitest fixtures.
pipeline_inputs.details: Create packages/scanner-utils/src/gs1-parser.ts with parseGS1/validateGTIN14/detectBarcodeType per §10.3. Handle GS (\x1d) variable-length AIs, Y2K convention, primary AIs 01/10/17/21/310x/3103/3922. Unknown AIs land in unknownAIs[] without throwing. Tests use a curated fixture file with ≥20 cases per primary AI plus edge cases listed in PRD §10.3.

Do not couple this to a server-only runtime (must be isomorphic)
Do not extend P2 AIs (13/15/SSCC) in this task
